qa.notes: Backward compatibility broken with legacy save files; dual storage consistency risks; rounding errors affect determinism; low test coverage for critical path; missing negative quantity validation.

security.notes: Missing input validation and bounds checking; inconsistent error handling leading to data corruption; unbounded growth risk.

devops.notes: Performance regression from O(n) sync operations; memory overhead from dual storage; migration compatibility risk for saved games with legacy resource strings.

sre.notes: Silent error handling in inventory operations; lack of observability (no logging/metrics); data synchronization failures with no recovery mechanism.

techlead.notes: P1: Fallback logic uses string literals but Type is economy.ResourceType; P2: Unused conversion package duplicates functions; dual storage system complexity; magic numbers and hardcoded strings.

docs.notes: P1: Legacy string comparison bug; P2: Event.Type mismatch; P3: Missing CHANGELOG entry.

promptops.notes: Language-specific assumptions in quality gates (Go-specific); CLI command inconsistencies; skill sizes within limits.

checks.note: Quality gates in AGENTS.md are Go-specific (go build/go test/go vet). Feature skill mentions targeting Go projects. Reduces reusability across languages.
All referenced CLI commands exist (sdp, bd, sdp guard, sdp quality, sdp orchestrate). sdp-orchestrate binary not in PATH but source exists and can be run via go run.
No vague handoff lists found. The handoff block in review skill is a specific command.
All 24 skill files are under 200 lines (max 191 lines).

summary: Feature F004 (Resource Economy System Integration) fails quality review with 10 P1 blocking findings across 6 of 7 reviewer roles. Major issues: backward compatibility broken for legacy save files, silent error handling in inventory operations, missing input validation, performance regression from dual storage sync, and fallback logic bugs. PromptOps passes with P2 findings only. All findings must be addressed before approval.
